item.title: Aviatrix Certified Engineer Associate
item.description: Exam Content As-A-Service Models AWS Networking AWS Organization Hierarchy AWS VPCs Azure Networking Azure Resource Hierarchy Azure Virtual Networks (VNets) Azure Virtual Network Gateways Azure Transit Options GCP Networking GCP VPCs GCP Cloud Interconnect Oracle Cloud Infrastructure OCI Resource Hierarchy OCI Virtual Cloud Networks (VCNs) Aviatrix Aviatrix Multi-Cloud Network Architecture (MCNA)

item.title: AWS VPCs
item.description: Example VPC Architecture Gateways Internet Gateway (IGW)—allows internet access from a VPC NAT Gateway (NATGW)—provides internet access to EC2 instances in private subnets, outbound only Transit Gateway (TGW) —Provides hub and spoke connectivity for VPCs Virtual Private Gateway (VGW)—Connects site-to-site VPN tunnels to a VPC Direct Connect Gateway (DXGW)—Connect global VPCs to Direct Connect Customer Gateway (CGW)—On-prem router equipment, connects with VGW, TGW or DXGW VPC Peering VPC-to-VPC communication Non-transitive Full mesh required for any-to-any connectivity Downsides: Difficult to manage firewall Unmanageable at high scale—125 peer limit

item.title: Transit Gateway (TGW)
item.description: Overview Hub and spoke connectivity for VPCs Default any-to-any communication Need to manage: VPC route tables TGW route tables VPC attachments Route table associations Route propagation Challenges 20 CIDR limit to advertise to on-prem DC 100 CIDR limit to advertise to on-prem AWS from on-prem Manual route table management No intra-region TGW peering Limited to inter-region peering support Each region—island No overlapping CIDR support Limited visibility No ping, traceroute, telnet etc.

item.title: AWS Application Modernization with A2C
item.description: Modernization Goals Accelerate business transformation Agility Costs Cloud performance Strategies 7 Rs: Refactor Replatform Rehost Repurpose Relocate Refactor Often default, but not always best approach Expensive—cost and time Replatform More cost-effective and faster Gain cloud experience in the process Refactor later Utilize containers Challenges of Containerization Technology Existing build processes—manual Lack of documentation Staff Lack of knowledge Low priority Operations Lack of expertise in containers/cloud Migration Steps Discover Prepare Build Launch Libraries Source code Dockerfile Infrastructure Dependencies e.

item.title: AWS Edge Computing
item.description: Why? Low latency Local data processing Data residency Use case: Online betting in the US—local regulations for data processing/storage locations AWS Hybrid Cloud Services Local Zones Outposts Wavelength How to Approach Questions to Ask Why can’t the workload go in region (i.

item.title: AWS App2Container (A2C)
item.description: Overview Migrate legacy apps into containers ASP.NET on IIS/Linux .NET Windows services Java on Linux—Tomcat, JBoss CLI tool—runs locally on instance Portable—on-prem, AWS Automated Scaleable—standard steps for all apps Source code not required—binaries packaged Follows AWS best practices for containerization Process Analysis of app: Dependencies Files Ports Containerizes with Docker Creates Artefacts ECS task definitions Kubernetes manifests CI/CD pipeline—AWS Developer Tools, Jenkins Cloud Formation templates Deploy to AWS Store image in ECR Deploy to ECS/EKS

item.title: AWS Elastic Container Service (ECS)
item.description: Overview Orchestration for containers Deploy, manage, scale Task Definitions Describes application—cf. Kubernetes manifest JSON file Up to 10 containers Includes ports, data volumes, launch type etc.

item.title: AWS Local Zones
item.description: Overview Subset of AWS compute infrastructure in Local Zones closer to metro areas Reduce latency for metros not near region

item.title: AWS Outposts
item.description: Overview Extend AWS infrastructure to on-prem Rack Fully-managed by AWS Available Functionality Compute ECS EKS EC2 Block and Object Storage S3 EBS EBS Local Snapshots Data RDS Elasticache EMR Networking Application Load Balancer (ALB) Outposts Server Use case: when full Outposts rack not required 1U and 2U servers Small workloads, e.

item.title: AWS Summit London 2022
item.description: Breakout Sessions Event Driven Architecture Continuous Resilience AWS Application Modernization with A2C AWS Edge Computing

item.title: AWS Wavelength
item.description: Overview 5G Use cases: require sub 5ms latency

item.title: AWS Control Tower
item.description: Overview Automate the setup of new AWS multi-account environments Best practices Managed service Replaces AWS Landing Zone solution Dashboard for monitoring compliance with guardrails across OUs and accounts enrolled in Control Tower ~1 hour setup: Create master account Enable Control Tower in desired region with configuration Creates two additional accounts: Log Archive and Audit Guardrails High-level rules for governance of multi-account environment Implemented with SCPs Preventative guardrails—stop actions, always compliant (except for accounts imported into Control Tower) Detective guardrails—detect noncompliance of resources, notify on issues, can be noncompliant Account Factory Allows creation of new accounts, and enrolment into Control Tower SSO Centralized and federated access management across all accounts Preconfigured or custom user groups Integrates with third-party ID providers (e.

item.title: AWS Multi-Account Strategies
item.description: Why? Many teams Isolation between workloads Security/compliance controls—resource boundaries Billing separation API limits/throttling Control Tower —orchestration framework for dealing with large numbers of accounts Best Practice Design Organization Master Account No connection to DC Manages OUs and SCPs Consolodated billing Contains minimal resources—no workloads, as these pose a security risk to the OUs and SCPs Foundational OUs Building blocks for lower-level OUs Security OU Accounts: Log archive Record everything happening in the accounts—CloudTrail, single source of truth Limited access—alarm on user login Security break glass Emergency access for security team Security read-only Security audit account, with read-only access to other accounts via cross-account roles Security tooling e.

item.title: AWS Organization Hierarchy
item.description: Overview Organizational Units (OUs) Group child accounts together Allow SCPs to be applied to multiple accounts Best practice: Group accounts by permission/policy requirements, rather than corporate structure

item.title: AWS Service Control Policies (SCPs)
item.description: Overview SCPs Applied at the OU level Controlled by the organization master account Restrict actions which can be taken in an AWS account Control which AWS APIs are accessible Allowlist/denylist Invisible to users in the child accounts

item.title: AWS Services
item.description: Compute Elastic Container Service (ECS) App2Container (A2C) Hybrid Cloud AWS Edge Computing Local Zones Outposts Wavelength Management and Security Control Tower Organization Hierarchy Service Control Policies Multi-Account Strategies
